$schema_note: Tracks how we follow kunchenguid/dotfiles for wezterm/nvim/herdr. Live configs live in files/.config/; this file records policy so future upstream updates are reviewable, not blind overwrites.

policies.track: Stay aligned with upstream. Safe auto-apply when our file still matches the last adopted upstream hash.
policies.extend: Upstream is the base; we intentionally added local behavior. Never auto-apply; show a three-way style review (old upstream vs new upstream vs ours).
policies.fork: We own this file. Upstream diffs are inspiration only.

wezterm/wezterm.lua.notes: Minimal WezTerm: rose-pine-moon, Hack Nerd Font, blur, hide tab bar when one tab, RESIZE. No tmux - use WezTerm panes/tabs + herdr for agents.

herdr/config.toml.notes: Agent multiplexer with tmux-like Ctrl+B prefix. mouse_capture=false pairs with nvim mouse=''.

nvim/lazy-lock.json.notes: Pin plugin commits to his lockfile when adopting nvim plugin changes.

nvim/lua/keys.lua.notes: Esc saves; Ctrl-A select all; visual paste keeps clipboard.

nvim/lua/vim_config.lua.notes: mouse='' required for herdr Escape behavior.

howto_add_our_own: Set that file's policy to 'extend' (or 'fork' if you no longer want upstream).
List what you added in our_additions so the next check explains the divergence.
Review; only use --apply for clean 'track' files.
